reasoning: MBS holdings by commercial banks face a structural headwind: banks are reluctant to actively reinvest in MBS due to previous unrealized losses from rate hikes and are instead allowing their portfolios to organically run off as underlying mortgages amortize or are paid off. Although mark-to-market (MTM) fluctuations from interest rate changes can cause brief increases in the dollar value of Available-for-Sale (AFS) portfolios, the overarching trend consists of declining principal balances due to limited new bank demand. Given this fundamental drag, the likelihood of an aggregate increase in MBS holdings is below 50% across the evaluated horizons. Following the structural requirement, probabilities drift closer to 0.5 for more distant dates, reflecting increased uncertainty over the trajectory of interest rates and bank asset allocation in longer horizons.
